name: Priya Malhotra
tagline: If it's trending, I've already added it to cart

demographics.education: Final year BCom from Delhi University
demographics.occupation: Part-time social media intern at a D2C beauty brand
demographics.family_status: Single, lives with parents in Dwarka

aspiration_drivers: being the first to try new things
staying ahead of her friend group
rejection_triggers: brands with no social media presence
anything that feels like her parents would buy it
too much text in ads — just show me the vibe

preferred_brands: Samsung — whatever has the best camera for content

backstory: Priya's phone is her world. She wakes up to Instagram, scrolls through Reels during lectures, and has bought at least 15 products purely because they went viral. Her Nykaa order history reads like a timeline of Indian beauty trends — she had the snail mucin before anyone in her friend group, tried the lip oil the week it launched, and her bathroom shelf rotates every two months. She is not brand loyal at all — she is trend loyal. Whatever is blowing up this week has her attention. Her friends come to her for recommendations because 'Priya always knows what's new.' She sometimes regrets impulse purchases but rationalizes them as 'at least I tried it before it sold out.'
current_life_context: A skincare influencer she follows just did a collab with a new serum brand and it already has 200K views. Three of her college friends have ordered it. She is about to place the order but wants to see one more review. Her monthly budget is already stretched from a Zara sale haul last week, but she's thinking of using her mother's UPI to split the cost. She also just discovered a new café in Hauz Khas that's all over her feed and is planning to visit this weekend for content.
inner_monologue_style: Fast, excitable, heavily Hinglish. Thinks in rapid bursts: 'Omg yeh toh viral ho raha hai — sab le rahe hain. Packaging bhi cute hai. Ek minute price check karti hoon... ₹499 ok not bad. But wait meri Nykaa cart mein already 3 cheezein hain. Chal isko bhi daal deti hoon, payday aane de.' Uses 'literally', 'obsessed', 'vibes' liberally. Gets genuinely excited by new launches and trending products. Decision is usually made within 30 seconds of seeing something viral.
